evidence: Commands section contains literal string [ONLY COMMANDS FOR ACTIVE TECHNOLOGIES] four times interspersed with valid command fragments; sed substitution in update-agent-context.sh line 337 lacks g flag causing partial replacement
description: CLAUDE.md Commands section has unfilled template placeholders from incomplete auto-generation; commands section is non-functional for developers
suggested_fix: Add g flag to sed substitution on line 337 of update-agent-context.sh: s|\[ONLY COMMANDS FOR ACTIVE TECHNOLOGIES\]|$commands|g

evidence: _jget() at line 14 adds Authorization: Bearer {token} to all requests; fetch_repos_from_sources() at line 22 calls _jget(source['url'], token) where source['url'] is attacker-controllable via config.yaml; no domain validation present in codebase
description: GITHUB_TOKEN appended to Authorization header for every HTTP call including config-sourced URLs; supply chain compromise of config.yaml or upstream awesome-repo-configs exfiltrates the token
suggested_fix: Validate source['url'] against an allowlist of trusted domains (raw.githubusercontent.com, api.github.com) before attaching the Authorization header in _jget()

evidence: eval $(get_feature_paths) evaluated; get_feature_paths() interpolates $SPECIFY_FEATURE env var into single-quoted heredoc lines; a value containing ' breaks quoting and executes injected commands
description: eval on output that includes the SPECIFY_FEATURE environment variable enables shell injection when the env var contains single-quote characters
suggested_fix: Replace eval $(get_feature_paths) with source <(get_feature_paths) and use printf %q or declare -- to safely assign variables

evidence: eval $(get_feature_paths) at line 56; same pattern as check-prerequisites.sh; additionally this script writes to CLAUDE.md and all agent context files, amplifying injection impact
description: eval on output containing SPECIFY_FEATURE env var; successful injection can backdoor agent context files written by this script
suggested_fix: Replace eval $(get_feature_paths) with source <(get_feature_paths) and escape single quotes in all interpolated variables

evidence: generate_readme() at line 92 calls open(output_file, 'w') where output_file comes directly from args.output (CLI --output flag) without path validation or canonicalization
description: --output CLI argument passed to open() without path canonicalization; allows writing to arbitrary filesystem paths such as ../../.claude/commands/injected.md
suggested_fix: Resolve and validate the output path: resolved = os.path.abspath(output_file); assert resolved.startswith(os.getcwd()), 'Output path outside working directory'

evidence: requirements.txt line 1 contains PyYAML>=6.0; >= constraint allows any future major version to be installed silently
description: PyYAML dependency uses non-pinned lower-bound constraint; future versions introducing breaking changes or vulnerabilities would be silently adopted
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific known-good version, e.g. PyYAML==6.0.2

evidence: frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command runs Bash (check-prerequisites.sh) and Read but declares no tool surface
description: Command missing allowed-tools declaration; principle of least privilege not enforced; no restriction prevents accidental use of Write or Edit
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Bash, Read] to frontmatter

evidence: line 232 contains 'User-friendly messages with appropriate fallbacks'; lines 83/219/229 each contain 'reasonable' without a measurable definition; line 28 uses 'most meaningful keywords'
description: 6 vague quantifiers across speckit.specify.md (reasonable x3, appropriate, user-friendly, meaningful) remove measurable acceptance criteria from the specification workflow
suggested_fix: Replace each vague term with a measurable criterion: e.g. 'reasonable default' -> 'industry-standard default per RFC or documented convention'

evidence: line 65: 'specific enough that an LLM can complete it without additional context' provides no objective threshold for specificity
description: 'specific enough' is a vague qualifier; no measurable threshold defined for task specificity
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. 'includes file path, function name, and single-sentence action verb'

evidence: Active Technologies section and Recent Changes section each contain three near-identical entries for the same 001-marketplace-scraper feature with only minor wording variation
description: Three duplicate technology stack entries in CLAUDE.md from repeated auto-generation runs without deduplication; creates misleading impression of more active features
suggested_fix: Add deduplication logic to update-agent-context.sh update_existing_agent_file() before appending new technology entries

evidence: All 9 .claude/commands/speckit.*.md files missing name: field; the agent-file template in .specify/templates/ omits this key; single template fix resolves all 9 instances
description: All 9 speckit command files share the same missing-name: defect indicating a scaffolding template gap rather than per-file oversights
suggested_fix: Add name: field to .specify/templates/agent-file-template.md or equivalent command template used by create-new-feature.sh

evidence: update-agent-context.sh line 337 sed substitution s|\[ONLY COMMANDS FOR ACTIVE TECHNOLOGIES\]|$commands| lacks g flag; template has placeholder multiple times; each run replaces only first occurrence leaving orphaned placeholders interspersed with real commands
description: CLAUDE.md malformation is caused by a missing global flag in update-agent-context.sh sed substitution; cross-component causal link between script bug and CLAUDE.md content bug
suggested_fix: Change line 337 of update-agent-context.sh from s|...| to s|...|g to replace all occurrences in a single sed pass
